positive_description: Left-lateralized mid-face and cheek deformation. A subtle left-side cheek compression or hollowing, with mild adjustment to the left nasolabial fold. The left side of the mid-face pulls slightly inward or tightens while the right remains relatively neutral. Creates a very subtle left-biased grimace or tension.
negative_description: Left-lateralized mid-face relaxation. The left cheek fills slightly and the left nasolabial area softens. The left side of the mid-face eases while the right stays neutral.
symmetry_notes: Asymmetric — this is the left-sided member of the psi17/psi18 conjugate pair. The 3/4 views show differential deformation between left and right sides, with the left showing more activity.
artifact_notes: No mesh artifacts at +/-3.0. Very subtle mid-frequency component. Safe at extended weights.
primary_face_region: left cheek, left mid-face, left nasolabial area
useful_for: bilateral mid-face expression (as conjugate pair with psi18)

interactions.psi18: CONJUGATE PAIR. psi17+psi18 at equal weights produces bilateral mid-face expression.
interactions.psi11: Same conjugate architecture — psi11/12 for mouth corners, psi14/15 for brows, psi17/18 for mid-face/cheeks
interactions.psi3: psi3 drives symmetric mid-face disgust/curiosity; psi17/18 provides asymmetric mid-face control

prose: Psi17 is the left-handed member of the third documented conjugate pair (psi17/psi18), operating in the mid-face and cheek region. The conjugate pair architecture continues the pattern established by psi11/12 (mouth corners) and psi14/15 (brows), now extending to the mid-face. Psi17 produces a subtle left-sided cheek tension or compression — the left mid-face tightens while the right stays neutral. Paired with psi18, the two halves combine into bilateral mid-face expression. The deformations are among the most subtle in this band, consistent with the decreasing variance captured by each successive PCA component.
